startups.title: OpenAI bets on families as ChatGPT goes deeper into households
startups.summary: More than three years after ChatGPT’s launch brought generative AI into the mainstream, OpenAI is broadening its focus beyond individual users to families. OpenAI is hiring a dedicated product manager in San Francisco to build experiences for families, caregivers, and older adults across its products. The hiring comes as ChatGPT’s audience continues to broaden beyond younger users.

startups.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
startups.summary: Meta has axed a controversial feature that allowed users to modify photos from public Instagram accounts using AI. Meta promoted one feature that allowed individuals to generate images by @-mentioning public Instagram accounts that they wanted to reference. Now Meta has reversed course.

startups.title: Bluesky’s interim CEO, Toni Schneider, drops the ‘interim’
startups.summary: In March, Bluesky’s longtime CEO, Jay Graber, stepped down from that role to become its chief innovation officer. Graber was immediately succeeded by Toni Schneider, the founding CEO of Automattic, which is the company behind WordPress and Tumblr. “I’m four months into my interim CEO role at Bluesky, and it’s time for an update,” Schneider wrote on his personal blog .

startups.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft

startups.summary: SK Hynix, a South Korean memory chip giant, said Friday it has raised $26.5 billion (KRW 40 trillion) in its U.S. market debut. But SK Hynix clearly isn’t suffering from the Korea Discount and that’s because it makes memory chips, including high-bandwidth memory (HBM). IPO for SK Hynix: Both Korean chipmakers just pledged more than $550 billion for new manufacturing investment in South Korea.
